title: crepis crocea subsp. dahurica
intro: Crepis crocea subsp. dahurica, also known as Dahurian Hawksbeard or Crepis dahurica, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb native to East Asia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Crepis crocea subsp. dahurica can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing individuals to harness its potential health benefits.
